mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with combined GDPC1 and HOUST growth (R-squared < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to inflationary pressures and reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by > 1% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% triggers central bank hawkishness, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to higher discount rates and yield curve inversion risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k indicates weak labor market performance, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced consumer confidence and spending.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS declines by > 50k.

mechanism_narrative: A positive correlation between consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with combined RSAFS and HOUST trends (R-squared < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising commodity prices (CPIAUCSL) signals supply-side disruptions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through stagflationary pressures.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with combined INDPRO decline and CPIAUCSL rise (Rank IC > 0.05).
